backend/main.mo: Motoko canister implementing tournaments, registrations, and user profiles with access control and various CRUD operations.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping high‑level features to affected frontend files and components.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Text file summarizing each frontend file's purpose within the application.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base styles and custom OKLCH-themed color variables for light and dark modes.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point mounting the root React app and loading main.tsx.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Sets up TanStack Router routes and wraps the app with theme and toast providers.
frontend/src/components/AppLayout.tsx: Root layout with navigation, page outlet, and themed footer.
frontend/src/components/AppNav.tsx: Navigation bar with responsive layout, authentication button, and dynamic link rendering.
frontend/src/components/AuthStatusButton: Button that handles login/logout using Internet Identity with loading state and toast notifications.
frontend/src/components/MyRegistrationPa: Shows the current user's tournament registration and allows cancellation if the tournament is open.
frontend/src/components/OrganizerRegistr: Displays a table of all team registrations for organizers, including team names, members, and dates.
frontend/src/components/OrganizerTournam: Provides organizers with controls to update the tournament’s status via a dropdown.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModa: Modal prompting new users to enter and save a display name.
frontend/src/components/RegistrationForm: Form for authenticated users to register a team, including team name and member IGNs, with login and status checks.
frontend/src/components/RequireAuth.tsx: Wrapper that blocks access until the user is authenticated, showing a login prompt if needed.
frontend/src/components/TournamentCard.t: Card UI linking to a tournament’s detail page, showing status, info, and prizes.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Creates and manages the backend actor based on authentication, invalidating dependent queries on change.
frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts: Simple hook exposing authentication state derived from Internet Identity.
frontend/src/hooks/useCurrentUser.ts: Hooks to fetch the caller’s user profile, role, and admin status using the current actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and provider for managing Internet Identity authentication, including login, logout, and identity persistence.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for fetching and mutating tournament data via an actor, including create, update, and filtered queries.
frontend/src/index.css: Sets up Tailwind CSS layers and defines light/dark theme CSS variables and utility classes.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Bootstraps the React app, wrapping it with React Query and Internet Identity providers.
A form page for authenticated users to create tournaments and submit data through the createTournament hook.
frontend/src/pages/HomePage.tsx: Displays hero content, stats, and a preview list of upcoming tournaments using fetched tournament data.
frontend/src/pages/MyRegistrationsPage.t: Shows the authenticated user's tournament registrations, rendering cards for registered tournaments or an empty state.
frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx: Profile page that loads the current user profile, allows editing the display name, and triggers setup if no profile exists.
frontend/src/pages/TournamentDetailsPage: Displays detailed tournament info, registration sections, and organizer controls, with loading and not-found states.
frontend/src/pages/TournamentsPage.tsx: Lists all tournaments with search, status and region filters, and renders tournament cards or loading placeholders.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading URL parameters, persisting them in sessionStorage, and clearing them from the hash.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind configuration extending theme colors, fonts, animations, and enabling plugins for typography, container queries, and animations.
project_state.json: Project metadata describing features, requirements, images, and current app state for the tournament platform.
